name: Wild carrot / carrot root (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (edible carrot root; wild carrot Daucus carota, composition taken from cultivated/wild carrot root, USDA + carrot food-science literature)

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Aprifel carrot sheet (INRAE/CIQUAL) https://www.aprifel.com/en/nutritional-sheet/carot/ ; USDA FDC 170393 total sugars 4.74
components.evidence: Sucrose is the dominant free sugar in carrot root (~4 g/100g raw); sweet taste is sucrose-driven

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free glucose ~1.1 g/100g raw carrot

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Free fructose ~0.9 g/100g raw carrot; sucrose>glucose>fructose

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Storage starch, secondary carbon reserve

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Renna et al. 2021 Sustainability 13(7):3940 (total organic acids 333.5 mg/100g FW, malate ~79%); Bureau et al./carrot-juice HPLC (L-malic predominant)
components.evidence: L-malic acid is the DOMINANT organic acid of carrot root (~79% of total organic acids); amount derived from total OA 333.5 mg/100g x 0.79

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Renna et al. 2021 Sustainability 13(7):3940 (quinate ~17% of total organic acids)
components.evidence: Second most abundant organic acid; amount derived from total OA 333.5 mg/100g x 0.17

components.name: citric acid
components.source: carrot-juice HPLC literature (citric ~5-10-fold lower than L-malic); Renna et al. 2021
components.evidence: Minor organic acid, 5-10x below malic; ~3% of total organic acids

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: metabolomic surveys of carrot root (present, unquantified) doi:10.3390/horticulturae11050542
components.evidence: Detected in carrot root but not reliably quantified; minor TCA-cycle acid

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Trace TCA acid, present but unquantified

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low protein root

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid; key to carrot taste/buffering

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: USDA reports serine; carrot free/protein serine is L-serine (D-Serine in FooDB list is a mapping artifact)

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: Also an organic acid

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Dominant carotenoid, provitamin A

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Ultratrace

components.name: molybdate (Mo)
components.source: present in carrot (FooDB); ultratrace essential micronutrient
components.evidence: Kept as legitimate trace element for growth (unquantified)

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Present counter-ion, unquantified

components.name: sulfate/sulfur
components.evidence: S source, unquantified

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of the (very low) lipid fraction

components.name: petroselinic acid (18:1 n-12)
components.evidence: Characteristic Apiaceae fatty acid; present, unquantified (no BiGG met -> uncovered)

components.name: total dietary fiber
components.evidence: Pectin/cellulose; largely non-fermentable by non-specialist microbes

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic pharmaceutical (contraceptive); biologically implausible in carrot; FooDB predicted/contaminant

remove.name: Gamolenic acid
remove.reason: gamma-linolenic acid is not a native carrot fatty acid; likely FooDB predicted (carrot lipids = linoleic/oleic/palmitic/petroselinic)

remove.name: Diosgenin
remove.reason: steroidal sapogenin characteristic of yam/fenugreek, not carrot; likely FooDB predicted/misassigned

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: plants contain L-serine; D-enantiomer entry is a database mapping artifact (replaced with L-serine)

missing_added: sucrose 4.0 g/100g (dominant sugar) with real amount
malic acid (~0.26 g/100g, DOMINANT organic acid - was absent)
full vitamin panel amounts (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B9, C, A/carotenes, E, K, choline)
full mineral amounts (K, Na, P, Ca, Mg, Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu, Se)
L-valine and L-phenylalanine (were missing from current list)
keep_uncovered: trans-isoasarone (genuine Apiaceae phytochemicals/volatiles; no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170393 (Carrots, raw; SR Legacy) - full macronutrient, amino-acid, vitamin, mineral and fatty-acid panel
Aprifel/CIQUAL carrot nutritional sheet https://www.aprifel.com/en/nutritional-sheet/carot/ (individual sugars sucrose 4.0 / glucose 1.10 / fructose 0.90 g/100g)
Renna et al. 2021, Sustainability 13(7):3940 doi:10.3390/su13073940 (carrot landraces: total organic acids ~333.5 mg/100g FW; malate ~79%, quinate ~17%)
Metabolomic/Transcriptomic quality-formation of carrot, Horticulturae 11(5):542 doi:10.3390/horticulturae11050542 (organic-acid profile: malic dominant, plus citric/succinic/quinic/fumaric)
FooDB FOOD00072 (wild carrot, Daucus carota) - source record
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for microbial growth on carrot root: sucrose (~4 g/100g) > glucose (~1.1) > fructose (~0.9), backed by malic acid (~0.26 g/100g, the principal organic acid) with minor quinic and citric acid; starch (1.43 g/100g) is a secondary reserve. Micronutrient/AA/vitamin panel from USDA 170393. Organic-acid absolute amounts are derived from a landrace total (333.5 mg/100g) partitioned by the metabolomic fraction (malate ~79%, quinate ~17%), so individual OA values are medium-confidence and variety-dependent; sugars, AAs, vitamins, minerals are high-confidence USDA. Removed heavy-metal contaminants (As, Cd, Pb, Hg), non-nutritive trace elements (Al, Ba, Sr, Si), an implausible synthetic drug (ethinylestradiol), and misassigned lipids/enantiomers.
